subject: Merge pull request #32 from ExLibrisGroup/release/private/david.bendavid/URM-317720
reason: Merge commit — its substantive content lives in 5eed863 and c713f83 which are evaluated separately. Cherry-picking the merge commit would duplicate that content.

subject: Auto-prefix assets/* URLs via webpack public path
reason: README-only docs for upstream's AssetsPublicPathDirective. TAU's README is structurally different (project docs, not framework docs) and the directive itself hasn't been adopted (decision pending on 5eed863). If we later adopt the directive, write fresh TAU-voice docs rather than cherry-picking upstream's framework-perspective text.

subject: NewImageDir: assets public path handling
reason: Skipped via upstream-sync; tracked separately as a future migration project (Option B: pull AND retire TAU's AutoAssetSrcDirective). See https://github.com/hagaybar/TAU_customModule/issues/8. If adopted, the directive will be re-introduced cleanly rather than via verbatim cherry-pick from this SHA.

subject: Merge pull request #30 from ExLibrisGroup/revert-29-release/private/david.bendavid/NewImageDir
reason: Merge commit for the revert PR (#30). Its content lives in 1350889. Even the underlying revert is irrelevant to TAU because the original buggy work (PR #29 / 0a7667f / aef3d33) was never on TAU — there's nothing here to revert.

subject: Revert "Release/private/david.bendavid/new image dir"
reason: Pure delete-and-cleanup commit reverting upstream PR #29's broken work (the 0a7667f conflict markers + the original directive file). TAU's fork never had any of that, so the deletions/patches have nothing to apply against — cherry-pick would fail or produce a confusing 'reverting things we never had' commit.

subject: Merge pull request #29 from ExLibrisGroup/release/private/david.bendavid/NewImageDir
reason: Merge commit for PR #29 — the original buggy attempt that introduced the 0a7667f conflict-marker mess. Upstream itself reverted this via PR #30 within 3 days. Three independent reasons to skip: (1) merge-commit mechanic, (2) underlying content is broken, (3) upstream rejected it themselves.

subject: Wire assets public path directive in prebuild
reason: BROKEN COMMIT — contains literal <<<<<<< HEAD merge-conflict markers committed into prebuild.js (~lines 46-50). Upstream itself reverted this via 1350889. Never pull this commit directly. The intended directive content was re-introduced cleanly in 5eed863, which is already tracked as a future migration in issue #8.

subject: Wire assets public path directive in prebuild
reason: README-only update (despite the misleading commit subject) describing how the directive ties into prebuild.js. Same skip rationale as c713f83 — TAU's README is structurally different from upstream's, and the directive itself isn't being adopted (Option B tracked in issue #8).

subject: third party version updates
reason: Skipped via upstream-sync. package-lock.json-only refresh (no package.json change); all 305 bumps are build-time/transitive/platform-binary deps (@angular-devkit, @angular/cli, ajv, @babel, @rollup/* native binaries) — nothing runtime/browser. A fork should refresh its OWN lockfile via npm update rather than adopt upstream's resolved tree verbatim. Low value, low risk.
